title: Capsella hians
intro: Capsella bursa-pastoris, commonly known as Capsella hians, is a small annual plant belonging to the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its heart-shaped seed pods and medicinal properties. Traditionally, Capsella bursa-pastoris has been used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Capsella bursa-pastoris include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Capsella bursa-pastoris is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as topical creams and ointments, which are used to treat skin conditions and wounds.
taxonomy: Capsella hians is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family. This plant belongs to the genus Capsella, which includes several species of annual plants. Capsella hians is native to Europe and Western Asia, and it is known for its distinctive flowers and seed pods. It is often found growing in disturbed areas, such as roadsides and waste ground.
